title: Bristol-Myers Squibb's Bright Future Unfolds Beyond Q2 2023 Turmoil
text: Bristol-Myers Squibb's revenue for the second quarter of 2023 was $11.23 billion, down 0.97% from the previous quarter and 5.6% from the second quarter of 2022. Sales of Opdualag, an FDA-approved drug for treating patients with unresectable or metastatic melanoma, totaled $154 million in Q2 2023, up 165.6% from Q2 2022. On the other hand, Camzyos, a cardiac myosin inhibitor developed by MyoKardia, which Bristol-Myers Squibb acquired for $13.1 billion in 2020, is seeing strong sales.

title: Bristol-Myers (BMY) Q2 Earnings, Sales Miss, '23 View Lowered
text: Bristol-Myers' (BMY) second-quarter 2023 earnings and sales miss estimates due to generic erosion for the oncology drug Revlimid. The company also cuts its forecast for 2023.

title: Bristol-Myers Squibb Company (BMY) Q2 2023 Earnings Call Transcript

title: Bristol Myers Squibb (BMY) Misses Q2 Earnings and Revenue Estimates
text: Bristol Myers Squibb (BMY) came out with quarterly earnings of $1.75 per share, missing the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $1.99 per share. This compares to earnings of $1.93 per share a year ago.

title: Bristol Myers Slashes Forecast on Tumbling Revlimid Sales
text: Cancer-fighting drugs Revlimid and Pomalyst drive a sales shortfall in the second quarter.

title: Bristol Myers stock takes a hit from earnings miss and lower outlook amid disappointing Revlimid sales
text: Shares of Bristol Myers Squibb Co. BMY, -0.56% shed 2.2% in premarket trading Thursday, after the drugmaker missed second-quarter earnings expectations and cut its full-year outlook, amid a more rapid than expected decline in sales of its cancer treatment Revlimid. Net income rose to $2.07 billion, or 99 cents a share, from $1.42 billion, or 66 cents a share, in the year-ago period.

title: Bristol Myers sales fall, cuts 2023 forecast as drugs face competition
text: Drugmaker Bristol Myers Squibb said on Thursday its second-quarter sales fell more than expected and cut its full-year forecast as two of its top drugs, blood cancer treatment Revlimid and blood thinner Eliquis, face generic competition.

title: The Bristol-Myers Squibb Phenomenon: A Game Changer In The Pharmaceutical Industry
text: On July 27, 2023, Bristol-Myers Squibb, one of the leaders in the global cancer and immunology therapeutics market, will release its financial report for the second quarter of 2023. title: Evotec and Bristol Myers Squibb Enter Licence Agreement within Neuroscience Partnership
text: EVOTEC AND BRISTOL MYERS SQUIBB ENTER EXCLUSIVE GLOBAL LICENCE AGREEMENT FOR SELECTED PROGRAMMES DEVELOPED AND PROGRESSED IN THE COLLABORATION SELECTED PROGRAMMES FURTHER STRENGTHEN THE PROGRAMME PIPELINE OF NEURODEGENERATION ASSETS EVOTEC RECEIVES $ 40 M PAYMENT AND IS ELIGIBLE TO EARN PERFORMANCE MILESTONE PAYMENTS, AS WELL AS TIERED ROYALTIES UP TO LOW DOUBLE-DIGIT PERCENTAGES ON PRODUCT SALES HAMBURG, GERMANY / ACCESSWIRE / July 11, 2023 / Evotec SE (Frankfurt Stock Exchange: EVT, MDAX/TecDAX, ISIN: DE0005664809; NASDAQ:EVO) today announced that Bristol Myers Squibb Company (NYSE:BMY) has exercised its option to enter into an exclusive global licence agreement. The licence covers selected late-stage discovery programmes that were developed and progressed within the collaboration.

title: Bristol Myers Squibb Is A Buy At Current Valuation
text: Bristol Myers Squibb is an attractive investment due to its consistent growth in sales and earnings per share, along with a generous dividend and aggressive buybacks. Despite facing challenges such as intense competition and high debt levels, the company's pipeline of over 50 drugs and therapies offers significant long-term growth opportunities. The company's low valuation presents a great entry point for long-term investors.

title: Bristol-Myers Squibb: Battling Exclusivity Headwinds
